rationale.R1.2: The artifact chains inter-judge kappa, variance reduction, and Spearman correlation into the conclusion that VisionPoLL is 'more human-aligned'; the logical validity of that inference—especially whether agreement/variance metrics actually entail human-preference superiority—is a plausible and central target of any scientific check.
rationale.R1.3: The load-bearing claim is that the panel better reflects human preferences, yet no human-correlation coefficient is reported; the supporting evidence (Fleiss' kappa, variance reduction, run-level Spearman) measures internal agreement and stability, not human alignment. Whether these metrics are sufficient to support the stated claim is a core check concern.
rationale.R1.6: 'Check this' is frequently used as a shorthand for proofreading or prose review, making writing polish an equally plausible primary scope alongside logical/evidence review.
